$comment: Per-app IDENTITY for WebAwesome. Copy to your app repo as drx.theme.json and run `drx-theme build`. Only these inputs vary per app; structure comes from @drx-dls/tokens.
$comment_customFontUrl: OPTIONAL (web only, DRI-108). A Google Fonts stylesheet URL, emitted as an @import so the font loads at runtime; reference the family from the fontFamily stacks above. e.g. "customFontUrl": "https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;700&display=swap". Native platforms ignore it.

formControl.$comment: Overrides WebAwesome's --wa-form-control-* group (inputs/selects/textareas/buttons). borderColor may be a hex or a variant-tint ref like 'neutral-70'.

components.$comment: OPTIONAL per-component background overrides. Omit a field to keep WebAwesome's default. Values are a hex or variant-tint ref like 'brand-40'. badgeBackground overrides wa-badge fill; formControlBackground sets --wa-form-control-background-color.

paletteOverrides.$comment: OPTIONAL per-step palette pins. Pin a variant tint to an exact hex, overriding the OKLCH-derived value for that one step. Shape: { "brand": { "40": "#abcdef" } }. Omit for a fully generated ramp.

devices.$comment: Per-device scale overrides. The palette and typography identity above is SHARED across all devices — only component sizes (scale knobs) differ per platform. These multiply the base device layer (tvOS = 10-foot, iOS = touch). Missing knobs fall back to the base device value. Use `drx-theme build --device ios|tvos` to export a standalone self-contained theme.<device>.css for a separate app repo.
